name: Cachalot (SS-170)
events: 7 Dec 1941 During the Japanese attack USS Cachalot (Lt.Cdr. Waldeman N. Christensen) was at the Pearl Harbor Navy Yard for a scheduled overhaul. She opened fire on the enemy planes with her machine guns.
12 Jan 1942 With her overhaul completed USS Cachalot (Lt.Cdr. W.N. Christensen) left Pearl Harbor for her first war patrol.
18 Mar 1942 USS Cachalot (Lt.Cdr. W.N. Christensen) conducted reconnaissance of Wake, Eniwetok, Ponape, Truk, Namonuito, and the Hall Islands before she returned to Pearl Harbor on 18 March 1942.

9 Jun 1942 During her second war patrol USS Cachalot (Lt.Cdr. George Alexander Lewis, jr.) patrolled north-west of Midway. On 9 June 1942 she put into Midway before continuing her patrol off the Japanese home islands.
26 Jul 1942 USS Cachalot (Lt.Cdr. G.A. Lewis) ended her 2nd war patrol when she returned to Pearl Harbor.

23 Sep 1942 USS Cachalot (Lt.Cdr. Harry Clinton Stevenson) left Pearl Harbor for her 3th and final war patrol. She was to patrol in the Bering Sea. After this patrol Cachalot was assigned to training duties.
23 Dec 1942 USS Cachalot departed the Panama Canal Zone for New London, Connecticut.
3 Jan 1943 USS Cachalot arrived at New London, Connecticut from the Panama Canal Zone.
30 Jun 1945 USS Cachalot departed New London, Connecticut for the Philadelphia Navy Yard.
1 Jul 1945 USS Cachalot arrived at the Philadelphia Navy Yard from New London, Connecticut for a refit.
